nodes.label: Melanoma in situ confirmed by pathology
Wide excision: Surgical excision with 0.5-1 cm margins (category 1)

Topical imiquimod 5% cream: For LM subtype when surgery is not performed, or for positive margins after surgical excision (off-label use). In a phase 3 trial of imiquimod vs RT for complex LM, 95% response rates at 6 months with comparable efficacy and safety [11].
Radiation therapy: For select patients with LM when surgery is not possible or desired
Active surveillance: May be appropriate for patients with poor functional status and/or limited life expectancy

flowcharts.title: Stage IA Melanoma (T1a: <0.8 mm, no ulceration)

nodes.label: Stage IA, T1a, Breslow <0.8 mm without ulceration, no adverse features. Risk of positive SLN <5%
Wide excision with 1 cm margin: Category 1. SLNB is not generally recommended unless significant uncertainty about adequacy of microstaging [5], [6].

flowcharts.title: Stage IB Melanoma (T1b: <0.8 mm with ulceration or 0.8-1.0 mm ± ulceration)

nodes.label: Clinical stage IB, T1b. Risk of positive SLN 5-10%. Includes T1a lesions with Breslow >0.5 mm and adverse features (age ≤42, head/neck location, lymphovascular invasion, mitotic rate ≥2/mm²)
Wide excision with 1 cm margin with SLNB: Category 1 for wide excision. Discuss and consider SLNB for these patients [5], [6], [12].

edges.label: Wide excision with 1 cm margin with SLNB

flowcharts.title: Stage IB (T2a) or Stage II (T2b or higher)

nodes.label: Clinical stage IB (T2a) or II (T2b+), clinically negative nodes
Wide excision with SLNB: Wide excision (1-2 cm margin for T1.01-2.0 mm; 2 cm margin for >2.0 mm). SLNB recommended [5], [6].

Adjuvant therapy for pathologic stage IIB/IIC: For pathologic stage IIB/IIC: systemic therapy and/or primary tumor site RT to reduce local recurrence (category 2B). Adjuvant anti-PD-1 (nivolumab or pembrolizumab) is approved. Shared decision-making required, weighing recurrence risk against approximately 15% risk of lifelong endocrine dysfunction [7], [13].

edges.label: Adjuvant therapy for pathologic stage IIB/IIC

flowcharts.title: Stage IIIB Microscopic Satellites (No SLN or SLN Negative)

nodes.label: Microscopic satellites in biopsy/wide excision specimen from primary lesion. Stage at least IIIB (N1c) regardless of SLNB status
Wide excision with SLNB: SLNB should be considered, especially if it will alter disease management decisions [14].
Consider adjuvant systemic therapy: For stage IIIB disease based on mic
